title: Meteorite Crater Discovered
dek: A 390 million-year-old meteorite crater was found using Google Maps
meta_description: Ancient meteorite crater discovered using Google Maps, Nintendo patent rejected
body_html:
A recent discovery has shed light on a meteorite crater that is approximately 390 million years old, as reported by Smithsonian Magazine. This finding was made possible by an amateur astronomer who utilized Google Maps to identify a strange indentation. The crater, which is a remnant of an ancient meteorite impact, has garnered significant attention and has been the subject of various news articles, including those published by USA Today and CTV News.

The discovery of this meteorite crater is a significant one, as it provides valuable insights into the Earth's history and the potential risks associated with meteorite impacts. According to Smithsonian Magazine, the crater is estimated to be around 390 million years old, which dates back to a time when the Earth's surface was vastly different from what it is today.

In addition to the discovery of the meteorite crater, there have been other notable developments in the world of technology and gaming. For instance, AUTOMATON reported that a Nintendo patent related to the Palworld lawsuit has been rejected by the Japanese Patent Office. This rejection has been met with significant attention, with many outlets, including GamesRadar+ and GameLuster, covering the story.

Furthermore, AT&T Newsroom announced that the company will be delivering more value with the new Samsung Galaxy Z8 Series. This move is expected to provide customers with more options and better services, as reported by AT&T Newsroom and PR Newswire.
